segments.text: If your logo is explaining a business,
it has already failed. The designer behind the NetJo and Chase logo revealed that a great logo only follows three strict rules. Rule number one, it
[music] has to be appropriate. It shouldn't explain your product. It just needs the right feeling. A sports brand feels bold [music] while a fashion brand
feels elegant. But having a right feeling is useless if people immediately [music] forget about it. That brings us to rule number two. It must be
memorable. Here is ultimate test. If you see a logo once, can you draw it from your memory on a piece of paper? If you can't, it's not memorable. But what
separates a good logo from an iconic one? It's the rule number three. It must be super simple. It has to look perfectly on a massive billboard and
just as clear on a tiny app icon. If it's too complicated, it literally falls apart on small screen. The real magic is keeping it simple yet distinctive. So,
what's your favorite logo? Tell me in the comments.
